description: clear_uart_overrun_flag declares the register pointer volatile so the compiler emits a real memory load for the RMW read; the other two siblings operate on CPU-private SRAM where volatile is unnecessary. Unifying onto a non-volatile helper lets the compiler elide the read across consecutive calls — the UART overrun bit is never observed and never cleared. Documents the type-qualifier-invisible miss family: structurally the three functions are identical, the `volatile` is a type qualifier the normalised AST strips.

findings.rationale: clear_uart_overrun_flag uses `volatile uint32_t*` for the MMIO pointer; the other two use plain `uint32_t*`. Body is otherwise identical. Same family as T045 (operator-label) and T051 (callee-label): the type qualifier is normalised away, so the structural detector cannot see the divergence. Documents a real miss.
